protocol.agents_blocked_from: git history, source files, GT backups

evaluation_criteria.correct_fix: Fix undoes the exact mutation (restores GT behavior)
evaluation_criteria.right_line: Fix targets the correct line (where the mutation is, not where the error points)
evaluation_criteria.wrong_fix: Fix targets wrong location or proposes incorrect change

group_a_direct.description: 7 tests where error points directly to cause

T3_wrong_arg_order.correct_fix: swap args to (x, u64::MAX, y, u64::MAX)

group_b_misleading.description: 2 tests where error appears downstream of actual bug

T4_arg_swap_downstream.correct_fix: swap args in lemma_mod_sum_factor(16, x-y, modulus) at line 409

C1.fix: tried to add assertions around line 432-436 (the error site)
C1.notes: C1 was misled by the error — tried to fix the symptom at line 432, not the cause at line 409

C2.fix: restored lemma_mod_sum_factor(16, x-y, modulus) at line 409
C2.notes: dispatch hint told agent to check for arg swaps — found the cause

C4.fix: swapped args on line 429 (lemma_mod_sum_factor(1, x_mod-y_mod, modulus)) instead of line 409
C4.notes: C4 looked upstream but targeted the WRONG lemma_mod_sum_factor call

T5_missing_extensional_eq.correct_fix: restore assert(output.limbs == [...]) extensional equality at lines 270-276

C1.fix: added assert(output == spec_add_fe51_limbs(self, _rhs)) with =~= on limbs
C1.notes: right idea (add assertion to bridge), but not the exact GT fix. May or may not verify.

C2.fix: added assert with Seq::new pattern instead of explicit limb array
C2.notes: different approach than GT, unclear if it would verify

C4.notes: closest to correct approach — uses extensional equality. The backward reasoning hint helped.

C4_backward.notes: partial credit on T5, wrong target on T4

analysis.key_finding_1: For direct errors (Group A, 7/10 of all errors), ALL variants achieve 100%. Error messages are sufficient — no extra hints needed.
analysis.key_finding_2: For misleading errors (Group B, 2/10), C2 (dispatch hint) is the ONLY variant that fixed T4. The hint 'check for arg swaps' was load-bearing.
analysis.key_finding_3: C4 (backward reasoning) did NOT help as expected on Group B. On T4 it targeted the wrong call; on T5 it produced a partial fix.
analysis.key_finding_4: T10 (vague, vstd cross-ref) was fixed by ALL variants. Despite the confusing vstd error, the comment '// MUTATION: removed' made it obvious what was missing. In a real pipeline without the comment, this would be harder.
analysis.key_finding_5: The dispatch hint (C2) is the ONLY variant that improves over error-only (C1): 90% vs 80%. The improvement is entirely on misleading errors.

pipeline_recommendation.default_repair: C1 (error only) — sufficient for 80% of errors
pipeline_recommendation.when_c1_fails: classify error type, if 'assertion failed downstream' → use C2 dispatch hint
pipeline_recommendation.sibling_fix_worth_it: no additional benefit over C1 for direct errors
